name: Daily Routine (Every Day — Training or Rest)

sections.title: Morning
items: Wake at fixed time (±30 min of target; consistent daily)
Get outside within 30–60 min of waking — 5–30 min sunlight on face/eyes (not through glass, no sunglasses)
Phone down until after sunlight exposure and morning movement
Take morning supplements with food: Magnesium (partial dose), B-Complex, D3+K2, Zinc, Selenium, Vitamin C (split dose), Creatine (partial), Boron (morning only — causes early waking if taken later)

sections.title: Throughout Day
items: Hit protein target: ≥1.6–2.2 g/kg BW across meals
Eat from animal base: red meat, eggs, liver 2–3×/week, shellfish/oysters every other day
Cook only in animal fats (tallow, butter, ghee) — zero seed/vegetable oils
Eat simple starches (white rice, potatoes) as primary carb source — no fructose, no liquid sugar, no soy
Walk 10–15 min after main meal (GLUT4 partial activation, lowers insulin faster, improves digestion)
No caffeine within 10–12 hours of bedtime
Drink filtered water where possible; avoid tap water for long-term daily use

sections.title: Evening
items: Last large meal 2–3 hours before bed
Blue light blockers on 1–2 hours before bed
Lower screen brightness; no social media scrolling in wind-down window
Reduce cognitive stimulation — no important tasks or problem-solving in the 1 hour before bed

sections.title: Pre-Sleep
items: Verify room temperature 15–20°C (60–68°F)
Blackout curtains closed, earplugs accessible
CBD oil if using: take 5 hours before bed (not immediately pre-sleep)
Sleep supplement stack ~30 min before sleep: Magnesium 300–500mg + L-Theanine 200–400mg + Apigenin 50mg ± Valerian 500mg ± Passion Flower 200–400mg ± Lemon Balm 200–400mg
Read for 15 min (paper book; no screen); insert earplugs; start ASMR/rain sound; attempt sleep

sections.title: On Waking
items: Wake at consistent fixed time (±30 min; body clock requires regularity)
Do not look at phone until after sunlight exposure and morning movement
Get outside within 30–60 min of waking

sections.title: Morning Sunlight (Protocol 2)
items: Go outside — do NOT get sunlight through glass, windows, or windshield
Look at the sky (not directly at sun) for 30 seconds; 2–3 sets
Duration: clear day ~5 min; cloud cover ~10 min; dense overcast ~20–30 min
No sunglasses (corrective lenses OK); sunlight on face and upper body
If waking before sunrise: turn on all bright artificial lights indoors; go outside at sunrise

sections.title: Morning Supplements (with first food)
items: Magnesium: partial morning dose (reserve remaining for pre-bed)
B-Complex: with food
D3 + K2 MK-7: with food (fat-soluble — needs dietary fat for absorption)
Zinc: with food (15–30mg; monitor copper if supplementing long-term)
Selenium: 100–200mcg with food
Vitamin C: split dose morning (3–5g total/day split across meals)
Creatine: partial dose (remaining split across meals; total 10–15g/day)
Boron: 3–6mg with morning food ONLY — never evening (causes premature waking)

sections.title: Morning Food
items: Protein-first breakfast from animal sources (eggs, meat)
If training today: protein-only pre-workout meal (zero carbs, fat ≤5–10g, low fiber) — see Training Day Routine
If rest day: normal first meal from base diet (protein + fat + optional starch)

sections.title: Morning (same as daily)
items: Wake fixed time; morning sunlight 5–30 min; morning supplements with food

sections.title: Pre-Workout (0–60 min before training)
items: Pre-workout meal: 30–40g lean protein only (whey isolate, egg whites, or lean meat/fish) — zero carbs, fat ≤5–10g, low fiber
Prepare intra-workout drink: 30–50g dextrose or cyclic dextrin + 2g sodium (Celtic sea salt) in water
Optional performance supplements 30–60 min pre-training: LCLT 2–3g; Citrulline 6–8g; Beta-Alanine 3g; TMG 2g; Creatine partial dose

sections.title: During Training
items: Start sipping intra-workout drink AFTER first 1–2 real heavy sets (not before; not during warm-up)
Continue sipping throughout session
Sodium during training: target ~2g/session (Celtic sea salt in intra drink)

sections.title: Immediately Post-Training (0–30 min window)
items: Warm shower ~10 min + slow/deep breathing with extended exhales (parasympathetic shift before eating)
Eat within 30 min of last set: 100–200g hot starch (white rice or freshly peeled hot potatoes) + 30–40g lean protein — fat ≤5–7g, zero fructose
With post-workout meal: R-ALA 300mg + HMB 1g + Taurine 2g + joint stack (MSM 2g + Glucosamine 2g + Chondroitin 1g)
Walk 10–15 min after post-workout meal

sections.title: 2–3 Hours Post-Workout
items: Second post-workout meal: animal protein + animal fat (beef, eggs, tallow/butter) — no carbs in this meal (GLUT4 tail has faded)

sections.title: Evening / Pre-Sleep (same as daily)
items: Last large meal 2–3 hours before bed; blue light blockers 1–2 hours before bed; sleep stack 30 min before sleep

sections.title: Morning (same as daily)
items: Wake fixed time; morning sunlight 5–30 min; morning supplements with food

sections.title: Nutrition Adjustments
items: Reduce carbs by ~25% vs training day (borrow those carbs for training days — same weekly total, different results)
Keep protein unchanged: ≥1.6–2.2 g/kg BW
Keep fat allocation unchanged; animal fats as base
No intra-workout carbs (no training session)
Eat simple starches with meals; keep fat and protein as training day

sections.title: Movement
items: Walk 10–15 min after main meals (maintain GLUT4 sensitivity, digestion, insulin management)
Light activity only — no heavy CNS demand on rest days; protect recovery

sections.title: Recovery Focus
items: Sleep is the primary rest-day tool — do not compromise on rest days
Use rest day for stress management: walk, sunlight, journal if needed

sections.title: Evening / Pre-Sleep (same as daily)
items: Last large meal 2–3 hours before bed; blue light blockers 1–2 hours before bed; sleep stack 30 min before sleep

sections.title: 5 Hours Before Sleep
items: CBD oil (if using): 7 drops 2% — take now, NOT at bedtime (stimulating effect reverses 3–4 hours after ingestion)

sections.title: 2 Hours Before Sleep
items: Blue light blocking glasses on
Lower all screen brightness to lowest setting
No more important tasks, stressful thinking, or phone scrolling
No more caffeine (should have stopped 10–12h before bed)

sections.title: 1 Hour Before Sleep
items: Easy activity only: reading, light conversation, low-stimulation
Begin cognitive wind-down — avoid problem-solving

sections.title: 30 Minutes Before Sleep
items: Take sleep supplement stack: Magnesium Citrate 500mg + L-Theanine 200–400mg + Apigenin 50mg
Optional additions (one at a time): Valerian Root 500mg / Passion Flower 200–400mg / Lemon Balm 200–400mg
Note: start with only Magnesium + L-Theanine + Apigenin — add others one at a time if needed
Verify room temperature: 15–20°C (60–68°F)
Close blackout curtains; set out earplugs

sections.title: 15 Minutes Before Sleep
items: Read paper book or e-reader on lowest brightness for 15 min

sections.title: Bedtime
items: Turn off all lights
Insert earplugs
Start ASMR/rain sound (YouTube or app)
Attempt sleep

sections.title: On Waking Unsatisfied (NSDR)
items: Do NOT look at phone or clock
Close eyes; let images play naturally; do not force sleep
Even without sleeping, the rest has recovery value (Non-Sleep Deep Rest)

sections.title: Weekly Food Anchors
items: Liver / organ meats: 50–100g serving, 2–3 times this week (vitamins A, B12, folate, copper)
Oysters or canned shellfish: 1 can or 6 large, every other day if possible
Bone broth: 1–2 cups daily or several times per week
Eggs: 3–10/day as tolerated — include throughout week

sections.title: Hair Protocols (if applicable)
items: Microneedling: once every 7–14 days (derma stamp 0.5–1.0mm; apply oil immediately after while channels are open)
Hair oiling: 2–3 times this week (rosemary oil blend or tallow; minimum 2h leave-on, overnight preferred)
Egg hair washing: 1–2 times this week (2–4 eggs, cool water rinse, 10–15 min leave-on)
Bentonite clay scalp mask: 1–2 times this week (10–20 min contact time; follow with oil or egg conditioning)
Wooden comb: 2–3 times daily, 50–100 strokes, gentle pressure
Red light therapy (if using device): 3–4 times this week, 10–15 min per session (660nm + 850nm, 6–12 inches)

sections.title: Stress and Recovery Check
items: Stress journal: write when carrying cognitive or emotional load — review any recurring stressors
Optional mood tracking: score energy/mood 1–10 daily; identify low-point patterns to act on

sections.title: Supplement Review
items: Note any supplements added this week and observed effects on sleep, mood, libido, energy
Confirm no supplement is causing disruption (insomnia, GI issues, mood swings, acne, water retention)
Boron: confirm morning timing — if taken in the evening it can cause premature waking
If on DHT starter stack: track morning wood frequency, resting heart rate, waist measurement

sections.title: Training Review
items: Review training adherence: were all sessions completed?
Review recovery markers: bar speed, sleep quality, resting heart rate trend
Adjust carb cycling ratios if body composition is stalling

sections.title: Before Testing
items: Do NOT test ALT/AST within 5–7 days of heavy lifting (false elevation from muscle damage)
Do NOT test hsCRP if currently ill or within 1–2 days of very hard training
Test fasting: 10–12h fast for glucose, insulin, and lipid panels
Test morning cortisol in the AM (before 9am ideally)
Book test for a normal week — not during illness, extreme training block, or unusual stress

sections.title: Core Lab Panel (Phase 1 Baseline)
items: Sex hormones: TSH, Free T3, Free T4, rT3, Total T, Free T, SHBG, DHT, LH, FSH, E2 (sensitive), Prolactin, IGF-1, AM Cortisol, DHEA-S
Metabolic: Fasting glucose, fasting insulin, HbA1c, C-peptide, TG, HDL, LDL, ApoB, TG:HDL ratio
Inflammation: hsCRP, ESR, ferritin + TSAT
Micronutrients: Vitamin D (25-OH), Zinc, Copper + Ceruloplasmin, Selenium, Magnesium (RBC), B12, Folate, Homocysteine
Liver/Kidney: ALT, AST, GGT, ALP, Bilirubin, Albumin, Creatinine, Cystatin C, eGFR, BUN

sections.title: After Results
items: Compare each marker to optimal ranges (not just lab reference ranges)
Identify which markers are off and which cluster of causes they suggest
Make one targeted change at a time; retest specific off-markers in 4–8 weeks
Do not chase individual numbers — read patterns across related markers
Note: trends over time matter more than any single reading

sections.title: Monthly Protocol Review
items: Review supplement stack: what is working, what to drop, what to add — one change at a time
Review sleep log: compare average quality scores, identify persistent issues
Review protocol adherence: which protocols consistently applied, which were not
Note physical changes: body composition, energy, libido, mood, hair density/shedding, skin

sections.title: Non-Negotiables (Every Day Regardless)
items: Sleep 7.5–9h on a fixed schedule (±30 min) — single highest-leverage free intervention
Morning sunlight within 30–60 min of waking — 5–30 min, outside, no glass
Protein target: ≥1.6–2.2 g/kg BW across meals — animal sources preferred
Zero seed oils in cooking or food prep
No sugar / liquid fructose / soy products
No caffeine within 10–12h of bedtime
Room temperature 15–20°C at sleep

sections.title: Minimum Supplement Set (If Only Doing a Few)
items: Magnesium: split dose — partial morning, remainder pre-bed
D3 + K2: with food
Zinc: with food (15–30mg)
Apigenin 50mg + L-Theanine 200mg: 30 min before bed (minimum sleep stack)

sections.title: Minimum Movement
items: Walk 10–15 min after at least one main meal
Aim for 8,000+ steps/day where possible

sections.title: Base Diet Rules (Every Day)
items: 80% of intake from animal foods + simple starch
Protein: ~2 g/kg BW; sources: red meat, eggs, shellfish, clean dairy
Fat: 1.2–1.8 g/kg; sources: beef/lamb fat, egg yolks, butter, ghee, tallow — zero seed oils
Carbs: 2–4 g/kg; sources: potatoes, white rice, properly prepared oats
Weekly anchors: liver 50–100g 2–3×/week; oysters every other day; eggs 3–10/day

sections.title: Training Day Food Timing
items: Pre-workout: 30–40g lean protein only — no carbs, fat ≤5–10g (30–60 min before)
Intra-workout: 30–50g dextrose/cyclic dextrin + 2g sodium — start after first 1–2 heavy sets
Post-workout (0–30 min): 100–200g hot starch + 30–40g lean protein — fat ≤5–7g, no fructose
2–3h post-workout: animal protein + fat — no carbs (GLUT4 closed)

sections.title: Rest Day Food Timing
items: Reduce carbs ~25% vs training day
Keep protein and fat allocations unchanged
Eat protein + fat meals; minimal standalone starch meals

sections.title: Foods to Eliminate Permanently
items: All seed/vegetable oils (sunflower, canola, soybean, corn, safflower, peanut, rapeseed)
All soy products (soy milk, tofu, edamame, soy protein, soy lecithin from processed food)
Liquid sugar (sodas, juices, sports drinks with sugar, alcohol mixers)
Pastries, candy, sweets, refined processed carbohydrates
Random supermarket dairy (use A2/goat/trusted source only)
Fructose as a daily carb source (fruit, honey used freely, HFCS)

sections.title: Meat and Food Prep Rules
items: Meat: soak in water + baking soda (5g/bowl) for 1–3h before cooking
Meat: cook in oven in own fat; rare to medium-rare — never overcook
Oats: soak, discard water, ferment ~24h in raw A2 kefir/yogurt before eating
Produce (if consumed): soak 15–60 min in water + natural soap or baking soda/vinegar

sections.title: Daily Hair Habits
items: Wooden comb: 2–3 times daily, 50–100 strokes, gentle pressure (distributes sebum, stimulates scalp circulation)
Morning sunlight: 20–40 min midday on maximum skin for vitamin D production (separate from morning circadian light)
Silk/satin pillowcase: reduces friction and breakage overnight
No conventional shampoo with SLS/SLES — switch to egg washing

sections.title: Weekly Hair Treatments
items: Egg hair washing: 1–2×/week — 2–4 raw pasture-raised eggs; apply to wet hair; massage 2–3 min; leave 10–15 min; rinse COOL water only
Hair oiling (carrier blend): 2–3×/week — rosemary (10–15%) + castor (40%) + coconut (30%) + olive/avocado (20%) + jojoba (10%); massage 5–10 min; leave minimum 2h; overnight preferred
Beef tallow scalp treatment: 1–2×/week — melt grass-fed tallow; apply to scalp; massage; leave 2–6h or overnight; wash with egg or clay
Bentonite clay scalp mask: 1–2×/week — 10–20 min contact; follow with oil or egg wash
Red light therapy: 3–4×/week — 10–15 min/session (660nm + 850nm at 6–12 inches distance)

sections.title: Every 7–14 Days
items: Microneedling: derma stamp or pen (NOT roller) 0.5–1.0mm depth; 3–5 passes per area; stamp until mild erythema
Apply oil or serum IMMEDIATELY post-needling while channels are open (absorption 10–100× higher)
Do not touch scalp for 6–12 hours post-needling to prevent infection

sections.title: Internal Support (Daily)
items: Vitamin D: optimize to 50–80 ng/mL (86% of men with pattern hair loss are deficient)
Collagen peptides: 10–20g/day with 1g Vitamin C
Selenium: 100–200mcg/day
Zinc: 15–30mg/day with food
Liver 2–3×/week: provides biotin, B12, B-vitamins, copper

sections.title: Shower Filter
items: Install multi-stage shower filter: KDF + activated carbon + vitamin C cartridge
Replace filter every 3–6 months
Chlorine and heavy metals in tap water stress the scalp and alter local androgen sensitivity

sections.title: Morning (with first food)
items: Boron: 3–6mg — MORNING ONLY (causes premature waking if taken in evening)
D3 + K2 MK-7: with food (fat-soluble; needs dietary fat)
B-Complex: with food
Zinc: 15–30mg with food
Selenium: 100–200mcg with food
Vitamin C: partial dose (split across 2–3 meals)
Magnesium: partial morning dose (remainder pre-bed)
Creatine: partial dose (10–15g total/day split across meals)

sections.title: Pre-Workout (30–60 min before training)
items: LCLT (L-Carnitine L-Tartrate): 2–3g
Citrulline: 6–8g
Beta-Alanine: 3g
TMG (Betaine): 2g
HMB-FA (if using fast-absorb form): 1g
Creatine: partial dose

sections.title: Intra-Workout
items: Dextrose or cyclic dextrin: 30–50g in water
Sodium (Celtic sea salt): ~2g
Optional: Potassium 500mg; Glycerin 10ml for hyperhydration

sections.title: Post-Workout (with first post-training meal)
items: R-ALA: 300mg (pure R-form only)
HMB: 1g (Ca-HMB or HMB-FA/BetaTOR)
Taurine: 2g
Joint stack: MSM 2g + Glucosamine 2g + Chondroitin 1g
Creatine: partial dose (glucose aids creatine uptake post-workout)

sections.title: With Meals (general)
items: Kestose (if using): 2–5g with meals — ramp up slowly (1g → 2g → 3g → 5g per meal over 2+ weeks)
L. reuteri 6475/17938 (if using): 1–2 caps with food
Sodium butyrate (if using): 500–1,000mg with meals
Digestive enzymes (if needed): with heavy meat or fat meals only

sections.title: Pre-Bed (30 min before sleep)
items: Magnesium: 300–500mg (Citrate, Glycinate, or Bisglycinate)
L-Theanine: 200–400mg
Apigenin: 50mg
Optional: Valerian Root 500mg / Passion Flower 200–400mg / Lemon Balm 200–400mg
Glycine (if using): 3–5g
CBD oil: 5 HOURS before bed (not 30 min — stimulating effect delays 3–4h before calming)

sections.title: Timing Cautions
items: Boron: morning only — causes early waking if taken in evening
Vitamin C: do NOT take at night — author experienced disrupted sleep
Forskolin: do NOT take at night — sleep disruption
Ashwagandha: only short-term stress cycles — not long-term; author experienced emotional blunting
Melatonin: not recommended by author for regular use (added only 3.9 min average sleep time in meta-analysis)
Caffeine: cut off 10–12h before sleep
